title: Solenostemon decumbens
taxonomy: Solenostemon decumbens is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint family. Solenostemon decumbens is further classified under the subfamily Nepetoideae, which contains many species of ornamental and medicinal plants. The genus Solenostemon is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Solenostemon decumbens is one of the species within this genus.
uses: Solenostemon decumbens, also known as Coleus, is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, the plant's extracts are used in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Solenostemon decumbens.

The leaves of Solenostemon decumbens have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
